items.title: Oekraïne baalt van 'verwoestend nieuws' Zinchenko en 'angstaanjagende hersteltijd'
items.summary: Niet alleen in Amsterdam, maar ook in Oekraïne is de blessure van Oleksandr Zinchenko hard aangekomen. Maandag werd bekend dat de multifunctionele verdediger er de rest van het seizoen uit ligt . Daardoor mist hij ook de voor Oekraïne cruciale WK-play-offs en – indien geplaatst – misschien het toernooi zelf ook wel.

items.title: Trots bij Macclesfield ondanks uitschakeling: 'We trainen twee keer per week'
items.summary: Macclesfield FC verlaat het FA Cup-toernooi met een meer dan opgeheven hoofd. De subtopper in de National League North (zesde niveau), die in de voorgaande ronde Crystal Palace elimineerde, kreeg volop complimenten na een nipte nederlaag tegen Premier League-club Brentford .

items.title: Mourinho looft Real Madrid-trainer Arbeloa: 'Niet voor iedereen weggelegd'
items.summary: José Mourinho staat dinsdagavond opnieuw oog in oog met zijn voormalige pupil Álvaro Arbeloa. De trainer van Benfica is lovend voor de voormalige verdediger, die volgens Mourinho over de juiste kwaliteiten beschikt om Real Madrid te coachen.

items.title: Is facing a Champions League play-off really that bad?
items.summary: The Champions League returns this week and some of Europe's biggest teams will be back in action a lot earlier than they would have hoped.

items.title: Lamine Yamal en Frenkie de Jong gekraakt: 'Zijn slechtste wedstrijd in lange tijd'
items.summary: Barcelona leed voor de tweede keer in vijf dagen tijd een gevoelige nederlaag en dat is volgens Spaanse media mede te wijten aan povere optredens van Lamine Yamal en Frenkie de Jong. Volgens Marca speelde eerstgenoemde zelfs zijn slechtste wedstrijd in lange tijd.

items.title: Fury: Usyk will beg me for a trilogy! 'Without belts, I'm still the man'
items.summary: Tyson Fury believes he will set a third fight with Oleksandr Usyk before the year is out.

items.title: 'We really messed up' - does European football need more balance?
items.summary: One set of clubs dominate the Champions League, another win their domestic title every year but cannot compete - does European football have a problem?

items.title: 'Man Utd aast op Mac Allister, Anderlecht polst Overmars'
items.summary: We zitten in een periode tussen twee transferwindows, maar de stroom aan transfernieuws houdt nooit op. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Button: Hamilton has 'perfect opportunity' to turn Ferrari story around
items.summary: The 2026 Formula 1 season provides Lewis Hamilton with the "perfect opportunity to turn it around" at Ferrari, according to his former team-mate Jenson Button.

items.title: Quebecois looks to get off the mark over fences at Newbury
items.summary: Quebecois faces Personal Ambition and Old Cowboy in Tuesday's feature from Newbury - live on Sky Sports Racing.

items.title: FA Cup: Wrexham to host Chelsea in fifth round
items.summary: Wrexham will host Chelsea in the fifth round of the FA Cup.

items.title: Luis Enrique slams 'worthless' Dembélé PSG take
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain coach Luis Enrique said he will not allow any player to be bigger than the club ahead of Tuesday's Champions League knockout phase playoff first leg against AS Monaco.

items.title: Own goal ends dream FA Cup run for Macclesfield
items.summary: Macclesfield were unable to recreate the magic of last month's FA Cup win over Crystal Palace, with a Sam Heathcote own goal sending Brentford through to the fifth round after a spirited contest at Moss Rose.

items.title: Klopp's agent: Man Utd, Chelsea made approach
items.summary: Jurgen Klopp's agent Marc Kosicke said his client had his pick of teams to manage after leaving Liverpool, including approaches from both the United States and England to take over as coach of their respective national teams.

items.title: Macclesfield FC's cup magic ends with an own goal, but with their legacy secure
items.summary: A second FA Cup shock always seemed unrealistic for tiny Macclesfield, but Monday's home defeat to Brentford does little to shake their legacy in this storied competition.

items.title: ‘It fills my heart’: Andrews lauds Macclesfield for their dream FA Cup adventure
items.summary: Brentford coach praises non-league side’s advances Manager John Rooney proud of Macclesfield’s efforts Keith Andrews made a beeline for the Macclesfield dressing room after Brentford’s fourth-round FA Cup victory at Moss Rose and told John Rooney and his players they had been immense against his Premier League charges. The Brentford head coach said footballing journeys like the one Macclesfield have enjoyed since their rebirth “fills my heart” as he waxed lyrical about the true test his team were given by the National League North outfit. Continue reading...

items.title: FA Cup heroes Macclesfield make memories to last a lifetime
items.summary: Macclesfield's FA Cup odyssey may have ended against Brentford but they have made memories to last a lifetime, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

items.title: Haji Wright hat-trick sinks Middlesbrough and returns Coventry to Championship summit
items.summary: “We are top of the league,” sang the Coventry City supporters on loop after returning to the summit of the Championship with a victory that quelled the nagging noise surrounding Frank Lampard and his promotion-chasing side. Coventry, pace-setters for the majority of the season, had won just four league games since the end of November. But Haji Wright hit a timely hat-trick as Coventry again traded places with Middlesbrough, whose six-game winning run came to an abrupt halt, to renew belief in these parts. Riley McGree pulled a goal back midway through the second half but from the restart Boro conceded a penalty that allowed Wright to claim the match ball. Coventry’s lead may be a single point but this felt a significant victory, psychologically as much as anything, their having taken just 16 from the previous available 39. “There have been quite a few questions asked and I think the lads should get a lot of credit,” Lampard said. “It was a big game, a really good game, which probably showed why we are one and two in the league. We have to take this as a bit of a template of what has to go into a game.” Continue reading...

items.title: Sky Sports Golf Podcast: Morikawa edges Pebble Beach thriller
items.summary: Listen to the latest episode of the Sky Sports Golf Podcast as Nick Dougherty joins the show to reflect on Collin Morikawa's win at the Pebble Beach Pro-Am.

items.title: FA Cup fifth-round draw: Mansfield v Arsenal, Wrexham v Chelsea – as it happened
items.summary: Wrexham and Mansfield host big guns in round five while Newcastle face Manchester City and Liverpool go to Wolves TNT have kicked off with a walk-and-talk around a packed Macclesfield dressing room, the only problem being that the camera lens keeps steaming up. Let’s get on with it, shall we? Three minutes until the draw, according to an on-screen countdown that will inevitably prove to be inaccurate. I think the fifth round is my personal favourite round of the Cup; close enough to Wembley but still with plenty of room for surprises. Continue reading...
